# discord Provider

Manage [Discord](https://discord.com) guild infrastructure — servers, roles, channels, permissions, members, webhooks, events and moderation — as code. One bot token manages every guild the bot is a member of.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    discord = {
      source = "kirchdev/discord"
    }
  }
}

variable "discord_token" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

provider "discord" {
  token = var.discord_token
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `audit_log_reason` (String) Reason recorded in the guild audit log (`X-Audit-Log-Reason`) for every change this provider makes. Defaults to `Managed by OpenTofu (terraform-provider-discord)`. Set to an empty string to disable. May also be set via `DISCORD_AUDIT_LOG_REASON`.
- `endpoint` (String) Base URL of the Discord REST API. Defaults to `https://discord.com/api/v10`. May also be set via `DISCORD_ENDPOINT` (mainly for testing).
- `token` (String, Sensitive) Discord bot token. The provider authenticates as `Bot <token>`. May also be set via the `DISCORD_TOKEN` environment variable.
